checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: Goodwill=216,444,000 (2009-12-31) rising to Goodwill=355,842,000 (2010-12-31) then roughly flat at Goodwill=383,719,000 (2013-12-31)
IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill=39,120,000 (2009-12-31) jumping to 72,269,000 (2010-12-31), then declining to 72,406,000 (2013-12-31) after peaking at 84,820,000 (2011-12-31)

Assets=6,259,830,000 (2009-12-31) growing to Assets=9,103,782,000 (2013-12-31), a business-wide asset base expansion consistent with a capital-intensive lease/rental fleet model, not disproportionate soft-asset growth

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: Depreciation=957,141,000 (FY2013) explaining the large, stable gap between NI and OCF typical of a fleet-leasing business

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: InventoryNet=50,146,000 (2009-12-31) growing to InventoryNet=64,298,000 (2013-12-31), roughly proportional to revenue growth over the same period
CostOfServices=1,662,303,000 (FY2009, 34.0% of Revenues=4,887,254,000) rising modestly to CostOfServices=2,366,820,000 (FY2013, 36.9% of Revenues=6,419,285,000)

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss quarterly figures for 2011 show meaningful volatility: 25,125,000 (Q1 2011), 40,033,000 (Q2 2011), 56,524,000 (Q3 2011), 48,095,000 (Q4 2011)
Revenues quarterly for 2011: 1,425,376,000 (Q1), 1,513,344,000 (Q2), 1,570,720,000 (Q3), 1,541,094,000 (Q4) show comparable seasonal variability to net income

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=13,808,000 (2009-12-31) growing to 16,955,000 (2013-12-31), roughly tracking the growth in ReceivablesNetCurrent from 598,661,000 to 777,370,000 over the same period

Liabilities=4,832,835,000 (2009-12-31) growing steadily to Liabilities=7,207,068,000 (2013-12-31), consistent with debt-funded fleet growth (DebtAndCapitalLeaseObligations=2,747,002,000 (2010-12-31) to 4,189,425,000 (2013-12-31))

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-K filed 2012-02-16 was followed only about a month later by a 10-K/A filed 2012-03-19 (accession 0001193125-12-121535), which supplied restated point-in-time figures for multiple prior periods (e.g., Assets=6,259,830,000 (2009-12-31), StockholdersEquity=1,345,161,000 (2008-12-31), Goodwill=355,842,000 (2010-12-31))

An earlier NT 11-K late-filing notification was made on 1996-07-01, and multiple CORRESP letters with the SEC appear across 2007-2013 (e.g., 2007-10-19, 2008-01-04, 2011-06-08, 2013-04-29), indicating periodic SEC staff review correspondence
evidence.location: Filing chronology - NT 11-K and CORRESP entries

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data points are available for Revenues, NetIncomeLoss, NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivitiesContinuingOperations, ReceivablesNetCurrent, InventoryNet, Goodwill, IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill, and AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent spanning fiscal years 2007 through Q1 2014

top_signals: A 10-K/A was filed just ~1 month after the original FY2011 10-K (10-K filed 2012-02-16, 10-K/A filed 2012-03-19), restating multi-year selected financial data (Assets, StockholdersEquity, Goodwill, etc.) back to 2007-2009, which warrants further inquiry into the reason for amendment even though no other misstatement indicators are present

Goodwill and intangible assets nearly doubled/almost tripled in 2010 (Goodwill 216,444,000 to 355,842,000; IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill 39,120,000 to 72,269,000), consistent with an acquisition, which also explains a temporary above-revenue receivables growth spike in 2011 (ReceivablesNetCurrent +22.7% vs Revenues +17.8%)

Core earnings-quality metrics otherwise look clean: operating cash flow consistently and increasingly exceeds net income (e.g., OCF=1,223,082,000 vs NetIncomeLoss=237,792,000 in FY2013), allowance for doubtful accounts grows roughly in proportion to receivables, and quarterly earnings show normal seasonal volatility rather than artificial smoothness
